perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Retail Promotions. 2.3.1 CNET will provide for various retail Promotions across the CNET Sites, which may include text/HTML links, buttons, portals and other fixed Promotions that include embedded links to the Company Site (the "Retail Promotions" and, together with the advertising banners contemplated in the preceding paragraph, the "Online Promotions"). 2.3.2 Subject to Section 4 below, CNET will provide the Company with a total of at least (a) [XXXXXX] Retail Impressions during the first three months of the Term, (b) [XXXXXX] Retail Impressions during the second three months of the Term, (c) [XXXXXX] Retail Impressions during the third three months of the Term, and (d) [XXXXXX] Retail Impressions during the fourth three months of the Term. For such purposes, a "Retail Impression" means the display of one page of a CNET Site that contains at least one Retail Promotion. If CNET fails to provide the Retail Impressions required by the preceding sentence during the Term, then CNET will continue to display Retail Impressions in accordance with this Agreement following the Term (notwithstanding the termination or expiration of the Term) until the required number of Retail Impressions has been delivered.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Retail Promotions. 2.3.1 CNET will provide for various retail Promotions across the CNET Sites, which may include text/HTML links, buttons, portals and other fixed Promotions that include embedded links to the Company Site (the "Retail Promotions" and, together with the advertising banners contemplated in the preceding paragraph, the "Online Promotions"). 2.3.2 Subject to Section 4 below, CNET will provide the Company with a total of Retail Impressions during the Term. If CNET fails to provide the Retail Impressions required by the preceding sentence during the Term, then CNET will continue to display Retail Impressions in accordance with this Agreement following the Term (notwithstanding the termination or expiration of the Term) until the required number of Retail Impressions has been delivered.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of specific impression targets for each quarter (a, b, c, d) and the definition of "Retail Impression" creates ambiguity. Section 2.3.2 now states CNET will provide a "total of Retail Impressions" without specifying the quantity for each period or defining 'Retail Impression', leading to uncertain enforcement of this clause. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the agreement now vaguely requires 'Retail Impressions' without defining what constitutes a Retail Impression and its value, yet refers to continuing to display them, creating an unenforceable obligation. The removal of the definition of 'Retail Impression' and the amount of impressions for each quarter creates the contradiction with the last sentence of 2.3.2. The last sentence states: 'If CNET fails to provide the Retail Impressions required by the preceding sentence during the Term, then CNET will continue to display Retail Impressions in accordance with this Agreement following the Term (notwithstanding the termination or expiration of the Term) until the required number of Retail Impressions has been delivered'. The preceding sentence does not specify any required amount or what is considered to be a 'Retail Impression' so the last sentence is contradicting because the last sentence states 'until the required number of Retail Impressions has been delivered'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3.2 Subject to Section 4 below, CNET will provide the Company with a total of Retail Impressions during the Term. If CNET fails to provide the Retail Impressions required by the preceding sentence during the Term, then CNET will continue to display Retail Impressions in accordance with this Agreement following the Term (notwithstanding the termination or expiration of the Term) until the required number of Retail Impressions has been delivered.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific impression targets for each quarter and the definition of "Retail Impression." This creates ambiguity because Section 2.3.2 now states CNET will provide a "total of Retail Impressions" without specifying the quantity or defining 'Retail Impression', leading to uncertain enforcement. The last sentence of 2.3.2 refers to "the required number of Retail Impressions," but the preceding sentence no longer specifies any required amount, thereby creating an in-text contradiction within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Reporting. Within 30 days after the end of each month during the Term, the Company will provide a report to CNET indicating the aggregate number of referrals from the CNET Sites to the Company Site during such month, the resulting number of buyers, the aggregate behavior (including orders and sales volume) of those buyers, and the total revenue attributable to the Online Promotions minus applicable sales tax, shipping costs, returns and cancellations (the "CNET Sales"). The "CNET Sales" will be counted as sales by the Company to each User who accesses the Company Site through a link from an Online Promotion for a period of 4 hours from the referral, CNET and The Company will agree on technical procedures to allow the easy and accurate reporting of CNET Sales. The Company will make this information available in a manner which allows CNET and the Company to understand the performance of the various Online Promotions.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Reporting. Within 30 days after the end of each month during the Term, the Company will provide a report to CNET indicating the aggregate number of referrals from the CNET Sites to the Company Site during such month, the resulting number of buyers, the aggregate behavior (including orders and sales volume) of those buyers, and the total revenue attributable to the Online Promotions minus applicable sales tax, shipping costs, returns and cancellations (the "CNET Sales"). The "CNET Sales" will be counted as sales by the Company to each User who accesses the Company Site through a link from an Online Promotion for a period of 4 hours from the referral. The Company will make this information available in a manner which allows CNET and the Company to understand the performance of the various Online Promotions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'CNET and The Company will agree on technical procedures to allow the easy and accurate reporting of CNET Sales', it creates uncertainty and potential disputes because it omits the crucial process through which accurate data is maintained. This creates an internal contradiction. The requirement to provide a report, as stated in Section 3.2, becomes unenforceable and difficult to comply with, because it does not specify how to agree on the technical procedures of reporting. This contradicts the purpose of having 'CNET Sales' data if there is no agreed method to track or validate those sales. The section still references CNET Sales, implying an understanding of data collection, but the agreed-upon mechanism for reporting CNET Sales is removed, creating an unenforceable provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Reporting. Within 30 days after the end of each month during the Term, the Company will provide a report to CNET indicating the aggregate number of referrals from the CNET Sites to the Company Site during such month, the resulting number of buyers, the aggregate behavior (including orders and sales volume) of those buyers, and the total revenue attributable to the Online Promotions minus applicable sales tax, shipping costs, returns and cancellations (the "CNET Sales"). The "CNET Sales" will be counted as sales by the Company to each User who accesses the Company Site through a link from an Online Promotion for a period of 4 hours from the referral. The Company will make this information available in a manner which allows CNET and the Company to understand the performance of the various Online Promotions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause 'CNET and The Company will agree on technical procedures to allow the easy and accurate reporting of CNET Sales'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the requirement in both the changed text and the contradicted text (which are the same) to report 'CNET Sales'. Without an agreed-upon technical procedure, the reporting requirement becomes unenforceable and lacks a defined method for accurate data collection, creating a strong contradiction because the parties would have no way to agree on how to implement the provision.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Cash Consideration. 3.3.1 For each month during the Term, the Company will pay CNET a minimum of [XXXX] in cash, plus [XXX] of CNET Sales. Payments under this paragraph will be based on the reports prepared by the Company under Section 3.2 (although CNET may challenge such reports as contemplated by Section 9.5) and will be due within 30 days after the end of each month of the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Cash Consideration. 3.3.1 For each month during the Term, the Company will pay CNET based on reports prepared by the Company under Section 3.2 and will be due within 30 days after the end of each month of the Term.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'minimum of [XXXX] in cash, plus [XXX] of CNET Sales' it creates uncertainty about the monetary compensation. It no longer specifies the amount or percentage of payment related to 'CNET Sales,' creating an in-text contradiction. The agreement references CNET Sales, but it doesn't explain how the amount of CNET sales is used in the consideration, thereby affecting and contradicting Section 3.3.1. By referring to Section 3.2 (which defines 'CNET Sales'), without the actual monetary consideration to be paid, creates the in-text contradiction. By removing the monetary amount and percentage, while keeping the base of the payment based on CNET Sales makes it unenforceable and unclear the means of payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3.1 For each month during the Term, the Company will pay CNET based on reports prepared by the Company under Section 3.2 and will be due within 30 days after the end of each month of the Term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific monetary consideration ('minimum of [XXXX] in cash, plus [XXX] of CNET Sales') while still referring to 'CNET Sales' as a basis for payment. This creates a strong contradiction because the contradicted text does not specify how CNET Sales is used to calculate the payment amount, making the payment terms unenforceable and unclear. It directly contradicts the principle of a defined compensation as the method of payment is not present in the change.
